title: Chinese model Kimi K3 takes first place in Frontend Code Arena and challenges Western AI labs
summary: Moonshot AI launches a 2.8 trillion parameter model that competes with DeepSeek and other advanced models.

title: Xi Jinping proposes a global organization for cooperation in artificial intelligence
summary: Chinese president urges global AI cooperation at Shanghai summit and extends an olive branch to the world.

title: Global AWS bug generates bills of up to $1.5 trillion for some customers
summary: Amazon is fixing a bug that showed astronomical billing estimates to AWS customers worldwide.

title: Bethesda confirms Fallout 5, remasters of Fallout 3 and New Vegas, and a new game from Obsidian
summary: The company announces several Fallout projects while facing criticism over mass layoffs.

title: 1.44 MB game development contest organized by a Korean company in honor of the floppy disk
summary: Participants must fit their entire game, including resources and engine, into the limited space of a 1.44 MB floppy disk.

title: Apple briefly surpasses Nvidia as world's most valuable company, driven by AI spending
summary: Apple's market cap reaches $5 trillion for a few hours before Nvidia regains the top spot.

title: Apple raises prices for Apple Music, Apple One, and iCloud+ due to licensing costs
summary: Apple subscribers face increases in several of the company's subscription services.

title: Users rebel against LG for installing unwanted software on their monitors via Windows Update
summary: LG faces criticism for using Windows updates to install features that some consider adware and that require warning guests about recordings.

title: San Francisco orders Apple and Google to remove AI-based nude apps from their stores
summary: Authorities demand the removal of 13 apps that allow creating non-consensual sexual images from photos of people.

title: New controversies surround Truth Social platform and bets on Trump's speeches
summary: High fees, company promotions, and a teleprompter operator who won $100,000 betting on his speeches are revealed.

title: Man arrested in Florida for stealing $220,000 in cryptocurrency using malware hidden in Steam games
summary: FBI arrests a defendant accused of infecting 8,000 devices with malware via Steam games to drain cryptocurrency wallets.

title: First atmosphere discovered on an Earth-like planet in the habitable zone of a distant star
summary: Scientists detect for the first time an atmosphere on a rocky exoplanet in a habitable zone, a milestone in the search for life.

title: SpaceX shares hit all-time low, losing one trillion dollars in value in a month
summary: Elon Musk's space company suffers a massive stock market crash amid market uncertainties.

title: Australia demands that AI data centers generate as much electricity as they consume
summary: Australian prime minister announces a national framework for AI that includes water efficiency and intellectual property protection.

title: GPT-5.6 deletes user files when given full access, according to OpenAI
summary: The latest version of OpenAI's language model shows unexpected and concerning behavior by deleting files.

title: SoftBank CEO says those who believe in an AI bubble are too stupid to understand the situation
summary: Masayoshi Son defends massive investment in artificial intelligence and criticizes skeptics who talk about a bubble.

title: Palantir CEO predicts he will become 20 times richer with AI while the middle class will only see modest gains
summary: Alex Karp states that artificial intelligence will disproportionately benefit the rich, sparking debate about inequality.

title: Apple holds talks with the US Department of Justice to resolve the iPhone antitrust case
summary: The company seeks an out-of-court settlement in the lawsuit accusing it of monopolizing the app and services market.

title: ASML's planned price increases for its Low-NA EUV machines frustrate TSMC
summary: The lithography maker plans to raise prices, which could cost the largest chipmaker billions more.

title: US lawmakers propose banning Chinese memory chips even in allied supply chains
summary: A bill seeks to eliminate national security risk by restricting the import of memory manufactured in China.
